mg_combat_rituel.score_rationale: Reference pattern fully implemented. cancelTimers() contract clean, double-guard present, no document-scope listeners. One structural assumption risk in cleanup() and one O(n²) loop allocation (non-blocking).
mg_combat_rituel.sfx_ingame_notes: Lines 270 ('lose') and 274 ('unlock') fire during render() — both correctly edge-triggered on collision state changes.
mg_combat_rituel.difficultyTier_notes: Lines 59-60 call this.tieredValue([16,14,12,10]) and this.tieredValue([3,4,5,6]) — totalTime and obstacleCount scaled correctly.

bugs.description: cleanup() body (lines 393-396) contains no teardown logic, relying entirely on a comment claim that super.cleanup() calls cancelTimers(). If MinigameBase.cleanup() is ever refactored to not invoke cancelTimers(), timers and rAF will leak silently. No defensive call in the subclass.
bugs.fix: Add explicit `this.cancelTimers();` as the first statement in cleanup() before `super.cleanup()`, making the subclass self-sufficient regardless of base class behavior.

bugs.description: Obstacle array rebuilt with spread inside for-loop (lines 129-138), allocating a new array on each iteration — O(n²) allocations for obstacleCount items. Harmless at n≤6 but contradicts immutable-update intent.
bugs.fix: Collect obstacles in a local `const segs: Obstacle[] = []`, push each item, then assign `this.obstacles = segs` once after the loop.

mg_ombres.score_rationale: endGame guard and difficultyTier correct. Two actionable bugs: cancelTimers() pattern not followed (duplication between endGame and cleanup), and missing pointerdown listener breaks mobile first-tap. Score formula has a fairness hole that lets wall-huggers pass at 50.
mg_ombres.sfx_ingame_notes: Line 217 fires 'lose' during render() on collision start — correctly edge-triggered via prevColliding guard.
mg_ombres.difficultyTier_notes: Lines 49-50 call this.tieredValue([16,14,12,10]) and this.tieredValue([30,26,22,18]) — totalTime and endHalfWidth scaled correctly.

bugs.description: endGame() (lines 188-191) and cleanup() (lines 350-353) both inline identical teardown code (clearInterval, cancelAnimationFrame, two removeEventListener calls) instead of extracting a protected cancelTimers() method as established by the reference pattern in mg_combat_rituel. Any future listener addition must be updated in two places — maintenance trap confirmed.
bugs.fix: Extract lines 189-192 into `protected cancelTimers(): void { ... }`, call `this.cancelTimers()` from endGame() and remove the duplicate inline teardown from cleanup() (let super.cleanup() call it, matching the mg_combat_rituel pattern).

bugs.description: canvas registers 'pointermove' but not 'pointerdown' (line 102). On mobile, the cursor stays at its initial position (30, canvasH/2) until the player drags — the first tap-without-move is silently ignored, preventing any score advancement. mg_combat_rituel correctly registers both (lines 109-110).
bugs.fix: Add `this.canvas.addEventListener('pointerdown', this.onPointerMove);` immediately after line 102, and add the corresponding removeEventListener in both endGame() and cancelTimers().

bugs.description: Score formula rawScore = maxProgress * 100 * (1 - collisionPenalty * 0.5) at line 196. A player who reaches 100% progress while colliding the entire run scores exactly 50 — sufficient to trigger the 'unlock' SFX (line 199 threshold: >= 50). Wall-hugging the full corridor length is rewarded identically to a clean 50%-progress run.
bugs.fix: Raise the penalty multiplier from 0.5 to 0.8: `rawScore = maxProgress * 100 * (1 - collisionPenalty * 0.8)` — a full-collision full-progress run scores 20 (lose), while a clean 50%-progress run scores 50 (unlock).

top_2_bugs.summary: Missing pointerdown listener on canvas — mobile players cannot advance progress from first tap, effectively scoring 0 on touch devices.
top_2_bugs.fix: Add `this.canvas.addEventListener('pointerdown', this.onPointerMove);` after line 102 and mirror in teardown.

top_2_bugs.summary: cancelTimers() contract not followed — teardown duplicated between endGame() and cleanup(), diverging from the reference pattern and creating a maintenance trap for future listener additions.
top_2_bugs.fix: Extract protected cancelTimers() and delegate from both endGame() and cleanup(), matching mg_combat_rituel structure.

cross_cutting.sfx_note: Both files correctly fire SFX during gameplay (not only at endGame): mg_combat_rituel lines 270/274, mg_ombres line 217. All are edge-triggered — no per-frame spam.
cross_cutting.difficultyTier_note: Both files read this.difficultyTier (via this.tieredValue) in setup() — totalTime and difficulty parameters scale correctly across tiers 0-3.

recommendations.action: Add pointerdown listener (OMB-02) — unblocks all mobile players immediately.
Refactor to cancelTimers() pattern (OMB-01) — reduces maintenance surface before next listener is added.
Adjust collision penalty multiplier to 0.8 (OMB-03) — closes wall-hugging exploit.
Add defensive this.cancelTimers() in cleanup() (MCR-01) — removes implicit dependency on base class implementation detail.
